# debtconsolidationvsbankruptcy.com

> The definitive guide to debt consolidation vs bankruptcy. Real information, no strings.

debtconsolidationvsbankruptcy.com is a free, open-source educational resource comparing debt consolidation programs with bankruptcy as debt solutions. Part of the Open Bankruptcy Project.

## Pages

- https://debtconsolidationvsbankruptcy.com/: The complete guide -- debt consolidation vs bankruptcy. When each works, when each fails, and what the industry does not want you to know.
- https://debtconsolidationvsbankruptcy.com/how-consolidation-works.html: How debt consolidation works -- consolidation loans, balance transfers, and debt management plans explained.
- https://debtconsolidationvsbankruptcy.com/how-bankruptcy-works.html: How bankruptcy works as a debt solution -- Chapter 7 liquidation and Chapter 13 repayment plans.
- https://debtconsolidationvsbankruptcy.com/cost-comparison.html: Total cost analysis -- consolidation fees and interest vs bankruptcy filing fees and attorney costs.
- https://debtconsolidationvsbankruptcy.com/credit-impact.html: Credit score impact over time -- consolidation vs bankruptcy. The 7-10 year myth debunked.
- https://debtconsolidationvsbankruptcy.com/success-rates.html: Completion and success rates -- fewer than half of consolidation programs succeed vs 95%+ bankruptcy discharge rate.
- https://debtconsolidationvsbankruptcy.com/who-profits.html: Who makes money from debt consolidation -- the industry exposed. Fees, commissions, and conflicts of interest.
- https://debtconsolidationvsbankruptcy.com/calculator.html: Interactive calculator -- should you consolidate or file bankruptcy? Compare total costs and timelines.
- https://debtconsolidationvsbankruptcy.com/when-consolidation-fails.html: When consolidation makes things worse -- warning signs, debt traps, and the consolidation cycle.
- https://debtconsolidationvsbankruptcy.com/faq.html: Frequently asked questions about debt consolidation vs bankruptcy with detailed answers.

## Key Facts

- Debt management plan (DMP) completion rates are under 50% according to multiple studies (NFCC, Cambridge Credit)
- Chapter 7 bankruptcy discharge rate exceeds 95% nationally
- Chapter 13 completion rates average 33-40%, but completed plans discharge all qualifying debt
- Consolidation loans do not reduce the principal -- they restructure payments, often at higher total cost
- Debt settlement companies charge 15-25% of enrolled debt and success is not guaranteed
- Bankruptcy provides immediate legal protection (automatic stay) that consolidation cannot
- Credit score recovery after bankruptcy often reaches pre-filing levels within 2-3 years
- The debt relief industry generates over $10 billion annually in fees

## Network

Part of the Open Bankruptcy Project:
- 1328f.com -- Discharge eligibility screener
- 1328f.org -- Research platform
- debtrelieforbankruptcy.com -- Debt relief vs bankruptcy
- debtsettlementvsbankruptcy.com -- Debt settlement vs bankruptcy
- creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org -- Credit card debt and bankruptcy
- meanstest.org -- Chapter 7 means test
- howmuchdoesbankruptcycost.com -- Bankruptcy cost guide
- chapter7vs13.org -- Chapter 7 vs Chapter 13
- automaticstay.org -- The automatic stay
- 341meeting.org -- Meeting of creditors

## Contact

This site is maintained by volunteers. No legal advice is provided.
